steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The phrase “gorgeous display” is decisive. “Gorgeous” describes something beautiful to observe, so “display” must mean a visible show or exhibition of the natural elements Mr. Bradford sees.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The word "display" needs to mean a visible show of natural beauty that Mr. Bradford perceives while driving. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Reproduction: "gorgeous reproduction of nature" — reproduction means a copy or duplicate, not a spectacle witnessed in the environment. ✗
  • Choice B — Concealment: "gorgeous concealment of nature" — concealment means hiding something, the opposite of a visible show. ✗
  • Choice C — Pretentiousness: "gorgeous pretentiousness of nature" — pretentiousness means showy arrogance, a human social quality that cannot apply to moss and cypresses. ✗
  • Choice D — Exhibition: "gorgeous exhibition of nature" — exhibition means a visible show or spectacle put on for observation, exactly what the landscape presents to Mr. Bradford. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
